india_trends.title: Inflation in driver’s seat likely to stall discretionary spend
india_trends.summary: While consumer demand had started improving over the past four months after a prolonged slowdown, rising crude oil prices, supply disruptions and Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s recent call for austerity could threaten to derail that recovery, said executives and analysts. india_trends.title: Containing war impact on growth, current account: PMO stitching up plan to boost foreign fund flow
india_trends.summary: Officials at both the finance ministry and Niti Aayog are identifying economic opportunities presented by the Iran conflict, as well as gauging the impact of the surge in global oil prices on the economy under different price bands. Niti Aayog will likely draw up a report on matter.

india_trends.title: Contrarian 5% Bet on 10-Year Treasuries Is Gaining Credibility
india_trends.summary: As US Treasury traders debate whether the 30-year yield will mount a sustained push past 5%, one market veteran is pushing a far bolder wager.

india_trends.title: US stocks today: S&P 500, Nasdaq end lower as inflation, Iran tensions weigh
india_trends.summary: US stocks closed lower as hotter-than-expected inflation and escalating US-Iran tensions weighed on sentiment. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq retreated from record highs, led by tech weakness, while Dow held steady. Rising oil prices and fears of prolonged inflation reduced hopes of Fed rate cuts and li

india_trends.title: Bridal jewellery gets a gold rush across India as panic buying over policy curbs and higher duties intensifies
india_trends.summary: Consumers are rushing to buy bridal jewellery across India. This surge follows Prime Minister Narendra Modi's call for austerity measures. Jewellers report a significant increase in sales. Customers fear higher import duties or GST on gold. Many are advancing purchases planned for later in the year.

india_trends.title: Outbound travel slows as Indians rethink foreign holidays amid crisis and costs
india_trends.summary: Airlines are experiencing weak forward bookings and muted growth plans due to high airfares and a weaker rupee, following a call to postpone foreign travel. While large-scale cancellations haven't occurred, the focus is shifting towards boosting domestic tourism and its economic benefits.

india_trends.title: GDP data release revised to June 7 each year
india_trends.summary: The statistics ministry has adjusted the release date for provisional GDP estimates and fourth-quarter data to June 7 annually, moving from the last working day of May. This change, driven by data availability delays, will see FY26 provisional GDP and Q4FY26 data released on June 5.

india_trends.title: India launches $1.5 billion maritime insurance pool, issues first covers
india_trends.summary: India has launched its significant Bharat Maritime Insurance Pool. The initiative aims to bolster domestic maritime trade by reducing dependence on foreign insurers. The pool offers a substantial sovereign guarantee, ensuring continuous insurance coverage for vessels. This move provides crucial assu

india_trends.title: Brazil Meat Left Out of EU Suppliers List in Risk to Trade
india_trends.summary: Brazil is absent from a list of countries authorized to supply animal products to the European Union, presenting a trade risk to the world’s largest beef and chicken exporter.

india_trends.title: Indian Banks poised for double-digit corporate credit growth on infra, energy, data centre push
india_trends.summary: Indian banks foresee robust corporate loan growth this fiscal year. Demand is surging from key sectors including renewable energy, infrastructure, and manufacturing. Major lenders like SBI and Bank of Baroda report strong loan pipelines. This expansion signals a positive outlook for business lending

india_trends.title: Voda Idea may consider raising fresh funds to bolster its books
india_trends.summary: Vodafone Idea's board will meet on May 16 to discuss a significant fundraising plan, aiming to secure fresh capital through equity and warrants. This move is crucial for the debt-laden telco to strengthen its financial position, fund network upgrades, and compete effectively against rivals.

india_trends.title: Power, renewable energy ministries to discuss green power curbs and grid penalties
india_trends.summary: Renewable energy and power ministries will convene Wednesday to address industry concerns regarding the deviation settlement mechanism and curtailment of green power. Stricter grid compliance rules have raised viability issues for renewable energy projects, prompting ongoing discussions and governme
